lean_stdlib_notes.no_list_get_question: List.get? does NOT exist in Lean 4.29 stdlib - use recursive listGet or List.get (Fin index)
lean_stdlib_notes.no_list_suffix: List.Suffix does NOT exist - use List.IsSuffix or <:+ notation
lean_stdlib_notes.list_issuffix_exists: List.IsSuffix exists with <:+ notation; List.suffix_cons, List.suffix_refl available
lean_stdlib_notes.list_set_exists: List.set and List.length_set both exist in Lean 4.29 stdlib
lean_stdlib_notes.list_replicate_length: List.length_replicate exists as @[simp] implicit args lemma
lean_stdlib_notes.no_by_contra: by_contra is 'unknown tactic' in Lean 4.28.0 - use Nat.le_of_not_lt instead
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg not available without Mathlib - use Nat.not_le.mp or omega
lean_stdlib_notes.no_split_ifs: split_ifs not available without Mathlib - use by_cases h : cond
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_min_pattern: For min bounds: use Nat.min_le_left/right. For equality: use Nat.min_eq_left (h: a<=b) : min a b = a.
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_max_le_mpr: Nat.max_le.mpr <h1, h2> proves max a b <= k from h1 : a <= k and h2 : b <= k
lean_stdlib_notes.barrel_file: formal-verification/lean/FVSquad.lean must import ALL module files for lake build to compile them
lean_stdlib_notes.import_placement: imports MUST be at the very top of the file, before any doc comments or code
lean_stdlib_notes.theorem_ordering: Define theorems BEFORE use - cannot forward-reference.
lean_stdlib_notes.rw_both_sides: rw [h] rewrites BOTH sides of goal. Use conv_lhs or congrArg (· % cap) h to rewrite only LHS.
